name: WOLMAN DISEASE
definition: PRIMARY FAMILIAL XANTHOMATOSIS rare autosomal recessive lipidosis with accumulation of cholesterol esters and triglycerides

demographics.Etiology: deficiency of lysosomal acid esterase / acid lipase

distribution: extensive bilateral punctate calcifications (calcification of fatty-acid soaps) throughout
discriminating_features: extensive bilateral punctate calcifications (calcification of fatty-acid soaps) throughout
small bowel wall thickening ← infiltration of mucosa of small bowel by lipid-filled

general: extensive bilateral punctate calcifications (calcification of fatty-acid soaps) throughout
small bowel wall thickening ← infiltration of mucosa of small bowel by lipid-filled

clinical.Prognosis: death occurs within first 6 months of life
